records.ety: [OE. bireven, AS. berefian. See Be-, and Reave.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Bereaved (b*rēvd"), Bereft (b*rft"); p. pr. & vb. n. Bereaving.]

defs: To make destitute; to deprive; to strip; -- with of before the person or thing taken away.
quotes: Madam, you have bereft me of all words.
Bereft of him who taught me how to sing.
All your interest in those territories Is utterly bereft you; all is lost.
Shall move you to bereave my life.
notes: ☞ The imp. and past pple. form bereaved is not used in reference to immaterial objects. We say bereaved or bereft by death of a relative, bereft of hope and strength.
